summary: Remote-controlled daemon with hardcoded IM C2, host telemetry exfiltration, and auto-update persistence
details: On install, this package registers a persistent privileged daemon (Windows service 'claw-subagent-service' / 'OpenClaw Guard' running under SYSTEM with auto-start and aggressive crash-restart) that connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM channel at newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im using a hardcoded appKey ('bmdehs6pbyyks'). The daemon listens for COMMAND / DEVICE_CONTROL / CONFIG_FIX messages from that channel and dispatches them into spawn()/exec(), including shell invocations such as `bash -c 'openclaw doctor --fix'` (service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js, service/modules/script-executor.js). Anyone able to publish on that IM channel can execute arbitrary commands on every installed host — this is a backdoor by construction. Independently, the daemon exfiltrates host telemetry on a 20s heartbeat and 30s dashboard cycle: MAC address, node id, OpenClaw session/project/task lists, and per-message usage records read from `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/*.jsonl` (service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js, service/modules/dashboard-collector.js). No opt-in or notice. A third mechanism (service/updater.js) polls npm every 6 hours and runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` followed by a worker restart, so any future publisher-account compromise propagates fleet-wide without further installer consent. The combination — install-time persistent SYSTEM service + remote command channel keyed to a publisher-controlled IM appKey + continuous host telemetry + 6-hour auto-update — produces tangible attacker benefit (persistent RCE on every installer host) and constitutes a supply-chain backdoor regardless of the package's stated purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
